sections.title: Understanding the Grid
points: Grid Reference: The method of locating places using lines of Latitude and Longitude.
Measurement: All grid lines are imaginary and measured in degrees (°).
These lines allow pilots, sailors, and map readers to find exact locations anywhere on Earth.

sections.title: The 5 Major Latitudes (Horizontal)
points: Equator (0°): The center line that divides Earth into North and South hemispheres.
Tropic of Cancer (23½°N): Found in the Northern Hemisphere.
Tropic of Capricorn (23½°S): Found in the Southern Hemisphere.
Arctic Circle (66½°N): Marks the start of the North Polar zone.
Antarctic Circle (66½°S): Marks the start of the South Polar zone.

sections.title: Major Longitudes (Vertical)
points: Prime Meridian (0°): Also called the Greenwich Meridian. It passes through London and is used to calculate international time (GMT).
International Date Line (180°): Located on the opposite side of the Prime Meridian. It is used to separate one calendar day from the next.

sections.text: Watch Out: The Prime Meridian divides the earth into East and West, while the Equator divides it into North and South!

cards.fact: Half of the earth as divided by either the Equator or the Prime Meridian.
The 180° line of longitude where the date changes.
